# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any // You can specify a particular agent, e.g., agent { label 'your-linux-agent' }

    parameters {
        text(
            name: 'ARTIFACT_URLS',
            defaultValue: 'https://cdn.example.com/app/v1.0.0/myapp.zip\nhttps://cdn.example.com/app/v1.0.0/config.json',
            description: 'URLs of artifacts to download (one URL per line). Supports HTTP/HTTPS via curl.'
        )
        text(
            name: 'TARGET_SERVERS',
            defaultValue: 'prod-web-01.example.com\nprod-web-02.example.com',
            description: 'Target production server hostnames or IPs (one per line).'
        )
        string(
            name: 'DEPLOY_USER',
            defaultValue: 'deployuser',
            description: 'SSH user for connecting to target servers.'
        )
        string(
            name: 'DEPLOY_PATH',
            defaultValue: '/opt/myapp',
            description: 'Base deployment path on target servers (e.g., /opt/myapp will result in /opt/myapp/releases/... and /opt/myapp/current).'
        )
        string(
            name: 'SSH_CREDENTIAL_ID',
            defaultValue: 'your-ssh-key-id', // IMPORTANT: Replace with your actual Jenkins SSH private key credential ID
            description: 'Jenkins credential ID for SSH private key to access target servers.'
        )
        booleanParam(
            name: 'SKIP_SERVICE_RESTART',
            defaultValue: false,
            description: 'Check to skip service restart after deployment.'
        )
        string(
            name: 'SERVICE_NAME',
            defaultValue: 'myapp',
            description: 'Name of the systemd service to restart (if not skipping). Requires sudo NOPASSWD for deploy_user.'
        )
    }

    environment {
        // Define a temporary directory for downloaded artifacts within the workspace
        DOWNLOAD_DIR = "${WORKSPACE}/downloaded-artifacts"
        // Generate a unique release name based on timestamp
        RELEASE_TIMESTAMP = sh(returnStdout: true, script: 'date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S').trim()
        // SSH options for non-interactive commands.
        // WARNING: StrictHostKeyChecking=no can be a security risk. In production, consider pre-seeding known_hosts.
        SSH_OPTIONS = "-o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null"
    }

    stages {
        stage('Prepare Workspace') {
            steps {
                script {
                    sh "mkdir -p ${DOWNLOAD_DIR}"
                    echo "Artifacts will be downloaded to: ${DOWNLOAD_DIR}"
                    echo "Deployment will create release: ${env.RELEASE_TIMESTAMP}"
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Download Artifacts') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def artifactUrls = ARTIFACT_URLS.split('\\n').findAll { it.trim() != '' }
                    if (artifactUrls.empty) {
                        error('No artifact URLs provided. Please specify URLs in the ARTIFACT_URLS parameter.')
                    }

                    artifactUrls.each { url ->
                        echo "Downloading artifact from: ${url}"
                        
                        // Extract filename from URL, handling query parameters
                        def fileName = url.substring(url.lastIndexOf('/') + 1)
                        if (fileName.contains('?')) {
                            fileName = fileName.substring(0, fileName.indexOf('?'))
                        }
                        // Fallback if filename is empty or only whitespace
                        if (fileName ==~ /^[\s\t]*$/ || fileName.empty) {
                            fileName = "downloaded_artifact_${UUID.randomUUID()}"
                            echo "Could not extract clear filename from URL '${url}'. Using generated name: ${fileName}"
                        }
                        
                        // Using curl: -f (fail fast), -L (follow redirects), -o (specify output filename)
                        // Make sure curl is available on the Jenkins agent.
                        sh "curl -f -L -o ${DOWNLOAD_DIR}/${fileName} \"${url}\""
                        echo "Downloaded ${fileName} to ${DOWNLOAD_DIR}"
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Deploy Artifacts') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def targetServers = TARGET_SERVERS.split('\\n').findAll { it.trim() != '' }
                    if (targetServers.empty) {
                        error('No target servers provided. Please specify servers in the TARGET_SERVERS parameter.')
                    }

                    // Get list of downloaded files to deploy
                    def downloadedFiles = sh(returnStdout: true, script: "find ${DOWNLOAD_DIR} -maxdepth 1 -type f -print0 | xargs -0 -n 1 basename").trim().split('\\n').findAll { it.trim() != '' }
                    if (downloadedFiles.empty) {
                        error('No artifacts found in the download directory to deploy.')
                    }
                    echo "Found artifacts to deploy: ${downloadedFiles.join(', ')}"

                    // Use sshagent for secure SSH key management
                    sshagent(credentials: [SSH_CREDENTIAL_ID]) {
                        targetServers.each { server ->
                            echo "--- Deploying to ${server} ---"
                            def remoteReleaseDir = "${DEPLOY_PATH}/releases/${env.RELEASE_TIMESTAMP}"
                            def remoteCurrentLink = "${DEPLOY_PATH}/current"

                            try {
                                // 1. Create target directories on remote server
                                // Also create a 'shared' directory if not present, common for logs/config
                                sh "ssh ${SSH_OPTIONS} ${DEPLOY_USER}@${server} \"mkdir -p ${remoteReleaseDir} ${DEPLOY_PATH}/releases ${DEPLOY_PATH}/shared\""
                                echo "Created remote directories on ${server}: ${remoteReleaseDir}"

                                // 2. Copy artifacts to the new release directory
                                sh "scp -r ${SSH_OPTIONS} ${DOWNLOAD_DIR}/* ${DEPLOY_USER}@${server}:${remoteReleaseDir}/"
                                echo "Artifacts copied to ${server}:${remoteReleaseDir}/"

                                // 3. Application-specific post-transfer steps (e.g., unzip, chmod, symlink config)
                                // This is a placeholder and should be customized for your application.
                                sh "ssh ${SSH_OPTIONS} ${DEPLOY_USER}@${server} \"echo '--- Remote directory contents on ${server}:'; ls -la ${remoteReleaseDir}/; echo '---'\""
                                
                                // Example: Unzip a specific artifact (if it's a zip file)
                                // sh "ssh ${SSH_OPTIONS} ${DEPLOY_USER}@${server} \"cd ${remoteReleaseDir} && find . -name '*.zip' -exec unzip -o {} \\;\""
                                
                                // Example: Make all .sh files executable
                                // sh "ssh ${SSH_OPTIONS} ${DEPLOY_USER}@${server} \"cd ${remoteReleaseDir} && find . -name '*.sh' -exec chmod +x {} \\;\""
                                
                                // Example: Symlink a shared config directory (create if doesn't exist)
                                // sh "ssh ${SSH_OPTIONS} ${DEPLOY_USER}@${server} \"mkdir -p ${DEPLOY_PATH}/shared/config && ln -sfn ${DEPLOY_PATH}/shared/config ${remoteReleaseDir}/config\""

                                // 4. Update the 'current' symlink atomically
                                // Creates a temporary symlink and then renames it over 'current' for atomic updates
                                sh "ssh ${SSH_OPTIONS} ${DEPLOY_USER}@${server} \"ln -sfn ${remoteReleaseDir} ${DEPLOY_PATH}/_temp_current && mv -Tf ${DEPLOY_PATH}/_temp_current ${remoteCurrentLink}\""
                                echo "Symlink updated on ${server}: ${remoteCurrentLink} -> ${remoteReleaseDir}"

                                // 5. Restart application service (if not skipped)
                                if (!SKIP_SERVICE_RESTART) {
                                    echo "Restarting service '${SERVICE_NAME}' on ${server}..."
                                    // This command typically requires the 'deployuser' to have NOPASSWD sudo access for systemctl restart.
                                    // Example sudoers entry: deployuser ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: /usr/bin/systemctl restart myapp
                                    sh "ssh ${SSH_OPTIONS} ${DEPLOY_USER}@${server} \"sudo systemctl restart ${SERVICE_NAME}\""
                                    echo "Service '${SERVICE_NAME}' restarted on ${server}."
                                } else {
                                    echo "Service restart skipped on ${server} as requested."
                                }

                                echo "Deployment to ${server} completed successfully."

                            } catch (e) {
                                error("Deployment failed for server ${server}: ${e.message}")
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }

    post {
        always {
            // Clean up the workspace, including downloaded artifacts
            cleanWs() 
            echo "Pipeline finished."
        }
        success {
            echo "Deployment successful!"
        }
        failure {
            echo "Deployment failed! Check logs above."
        }
    }
}
